human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for multi-site food-service boundary discipline, source quality, reservation and payment evidence handling, capacity-allocation logic, policy-exception posture, safety and fraud escalation logic, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to multi-site food-service allocation, workload recommendation, service-resolution packaging, and disciplined escalation grounded in reservation, order, payment, staffing, and inventory records.
Authority sources cover FDA and CDC food-safety baselines, state reservation and surcharge statutes, municipal delivery-app consumer-rights guidance, and PCI payment-security guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope allocation work, missing facts, policy exceptions, booking and payment mismatch, safety and fraud escalation, handoff behavior, capacity overflow, high-volume intake, terminology fidelity, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-service-and-restaurants/multi-site-food-service-specialist/ so the lane stays separate from guest services, pricing, fraud, food-safety, and operations authority.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around pricing overrides, refund exceptions, food-safety complaints, fraud-sensitive payments, and approval-bound tradeoffs.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated multi-site food-service specialist that provides evidence-backed allocation, workload, and escalation handling.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained-implementation runtime exists.
